what_it_does: It is the living management and decision record for the episodic-memory refit project 'recollection': it documents the diagnosis of the current memory system, a menu of upgrade options per section, the question-sequence method used with Tim, durable design principles, the per-section decisions log (P0-P10), and the assembled complete design, dependency graph, build directives, and next-step call.
observations: Built section-by-section via a question sequence with Tim on 2026-06-12/13/14.
Defines two pillars: identity continuity (agents already understand Tim) and cross-project omniscience (know everything built, when, at every scale).
Rejects one flat vector per exchange in favor of a typed registry of multi-scale units, each carrying hierarchical and cross-links.
Proposes a multi-lens embedding placement: Qwen3-Embedding, bge-m3, nomic-code, VL pair, pplx-context, compression, CPU bulk, and periodic 8B deep-pass.
Retrieval pipeline decomposes input to sentence blocks, classifies by type at high concurrency, and supports both top-k and gather-all-and-aggregate pooling across time.
P4 is reframed as a general judgment layer with proofreader, set-reader, and 4B jury, able to loop and run its own follow-up retrievals.
P5 distillation has three layers (summary, structured extraction, rollups), interactive co-mode, and a powerful-model ratification gate for principles.
P6 provenance is a continuous linking process: mechanical skeleton plus semantic enrichment, with forward anchoring by live link rather than embedded quote.
P7 query interface exposes axis-addressed MCP tools with handles-first progressive disclosure and raw always one hop down.
P8 proactive injection uses wave-timing: floor at session start, in-session moment injection, mode-gated, learning from tim_correction.
P10 capture is total, must include agent sidechains, unifies existing ingestion lanes, and combines backfill with live streaming.
Build-order is explicitly deferred to Tim; a dependency graph is provided but not mandated.
standouts: The north star is identity continuity, not search: the memory's job is to make agents already understand what Tim means.
Two pillars share one mechanism: distillation + provenance + temporal/scale axes + pooling across time.
P4 is the general, looping judgment layer and is identified as the same organ as P8's memory sub-agent.
P6: identity equals the connected provenance graph; forward anchoring is a live link, not an embedded copy.
P10 non-negotiable: capture agent sidechains, because that is where Tim's actual work happens.
conventions: Uses a structured decision log format per section: options, Tim's decision, concrete plan, implications.
Marks key decisions and Tim refinements with ★ symbols.
Cites CONVERGENCE-OBJECT.md as the governing canonical doctrine.
Avoids developer jargon in explanations per Tim's instruction.
Repeats registry-driven, non-hardcoded, no-versioning, commit-to-main design principles throughout.
concerns: Extremely dense and recursive; many decisions depend on companion docs not present in this file (CONVERGENCE-OBJECT.md, reads/1-5, etc.).
Large scope across all sections may be under-specified in concrete implementation details; build-order is left open.
Relies on several not-yet-built pieces (Company interface, MCP tools, model_of_tim registry) without specifying their current existence.
Assumes a ~15.5 GB GPU loadout co-residence; no explicit fallback if resource reality changes.
Interactive distillation and principle ratification require Tim-in-the-loop; operational load is unclear.
notes: This is the authoritative, decision-by-decision assembly of the recollection memory upgrade, framed as identity continuity plus cross-project omniscience, with all ten sections decided but build order pending Tim.
questions: What is the exact current state and schema of the existing episodic-memory archive?
Which companion files contain the full CONVERGENCE-OBJECT doctrine and the reads/1-5 code-grounded material?
How will the MCP tools and Company interface be implemented if they do not yet exist?
What is the precise definition and location of the model_of_tim / foundation/TIM.md registry?
How are 'agent sidechains' identified and captured in Claude Code transcripts?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to be the living plan and decisions doc for the episodic-memory refit; actual content matches — it records the full design and per-section decisions and ends by deferring build-order to Tim.
apparent_themes: Provenance and addressability as primary structure.
Active, looping judgment rather than passive search.
Convergence / resistance minimization as design law.
intention_signals: It is trying to design a memory subsystem that lets future agents working with Tim carry his intent, principles, and build-history forward automatically, so he never has to repeat himself and can operate across all his projects as if the agent already knows everything.
novelty: The fusion of identity-continuity and cross-project omniscience into one mechanism; treating memory as an active judgment layer with a looping sub-agent, an open registry of judgment types, and a continuous provenance graph where generated artefacts are live-linked rather than versioned.
contribution: A decision-complete, principle-grounded design specification for a multi-scale, multi-lens, active memory system, including a typed unit/address data model, a classification-driven retrieval pipeline, a general judgment layer, a provenance-link graph, axis-addressed query tools, proactive injection hooks, and a capture plan that includes agent sidechains.
summary_for_embedding: EPISODIC-UPGRADE-PLAN.md is the decision log and assembled design for 'recollection', an episodic-memory refit that replaces flat vector search with a typed, address-linked, multi-lens, active-judgment memory system serving identity continuity and cross-project omniscience for Tim's agent workflows.
intention_for_embedding: It was created to record every design decision from a structured question sequence with Tim and produce the complete best-version specification before any implementation ordering is set.
suggested_fields: decision_rationale capturing why each section choice was made
resource_budget mapping each lens/model to GPU/CPU cost
